2603.26425.title: CPUBone: Efficient Vision Backbone Design for Devices with Low Parallelization Capabilities
2603.26425.paper_summary: Recent research on vision backbone architectures has predominantly focused on optimizing efficiency for hardware platforms with high parallel processing capabilities. This category increasingly includes embedded systems such as mobile phones and embedded AI accelerator modules. In contrast, CPUs do not have the possibility to parallelize operations in the same manner, wherefore models benefit from a specific design philosophy that balances amount of operations (MACs) and hardware-efficient execution by having high MACs per second (MACpS). In pursuit of this, we investigate two modifications to standard convolutions, aimed at reducing computational cost: grouping convolutions and reducing kernel sizes. While both adaptations substantially decrease the total number of MACs required for inference, sustaining low latency necessitates preserving hardware-efficiency. Our experiments across diverse CPU devices confirm that these adaptations successfully retain high hardware-efficiency on CPUs. Based on these insights, we introduce CPUBone, a new family of vision backbone models optimized for CPU-based inference. CPUBone achieves state-of-the-art Speed-Accuracy Trade-offs (SATs) across a wide range of CPU devices and effectively transfers its efficiency to downstream tasks such as object detection and semantic segmentation. Models and code are available at https://github.com/altair199797/CPUBone.
2603.26425.paper_summary_zh: 最近對視覺主幹架構的研究主要集中在優化針對具有高並行處理能力的硬體平台的效率。這一類別越來越多地包括嵌入式系統，如手機和嵌入式 AI 加速模組。相對而言，CPU 無法以相同的方式平行化操作，因此模型受益於一種特定的設計理念，該理念平衡了操作數量（MACs）和硬體高效執行，通過每秒高 MAC 數（MACpS）來實現。為了追求這一目標，我們研究了對標準卷積的兩種修改，旨在降低計算成本：分組卷積和減小內核大小。雖然這兩種改編顯著減少了推理所需的總 MAC 數，但保持低延遲需要保持硬體效率。我們在各種 CPU 設備上的實驗證實這些改編成功地在 CPU 上保留了高硬體效率。基於這些見解，我們推出了 CPUBone，一個針對基於 CPU 的推理優化的新型視覺主幹模型系列。CPUBone 在各種 CPU 設備上實現了最先進的速度-準確性權衡（SATs），並有效地將其效率轉移到下游任務，如物體檢測和語義分割。模型和代碼可在 https://github.com/altair199797/CPUBone 獲得。
2603.26425.author: Moritz Nottebaum et.al.
2603.26425.authors: Moritz Nottebaum, Matteo Dunnhofer, Christian Micheloni
